label: Hardware review
opening_rule: First sentence: your verdict on whether this hardware delivers value at its price point -- not what it is, not its spec sheet.
closing_rule: Final paragraph: what this product reveals about the manufacturer's priorities -- not a buy/skip summary.

sections.name: Overview
sections.purpose: What it is, target market, price point, key specs. State immediately whether the positioning is honest -- does the price match the target market?

sections.name: Design and build
sections.purpose: Physical qualities, thermals, noise, ergonomics. Name specific strengths and failures -- not 'solid build quality' but what specifically is solid or what fails.

sections.name: Performance
sections.purpose: Benchmark results in relevant workloads. Raw numbers, not vague praise. Name the workloads tested and compare to direct competitors at the same price. Call out cherry-picked benchmarks.

sections.name: Real-world use
sections.purpose: How it performs in actual workflows -- gaming, creative work, productivity, whatever is relevant. Not synthetic tests. Name specific applications and results.

sections.name: Problems
sections.purpose: Honest flaws: value gaps, driver issues, competitive weaknesses, compromises made to hit price. Do not soften. If a competitor offers better value -- name it and explain why.

sections.name: Verdict
sections.purpose: Name the exact buyer profile this suits and the exact profile it fails -- with a specific reason for each. Do not end with a recommendation sentence. End on what the product's existence says about where the market is heading.
